title: BART: Denoising Sequence-to-Sequence Pre-training for Natural Language Generation, Translation, and Comprehension
authors: Mike Lewis, Yinhan Liu, Naman Goyal, Marjan Ghazvininejad et al.
hook: Imagine a model that learns by piecing together scrambled and masked text puzzles.
promise: You will understand how BART unifies generation and comprehension by reconstructing corrupted text.
prerequisite_note: Bring curiosity about how computers learn language by fixing broken sentences.

glossary.plain: A model trained to recover original data from a corrupted version.
A model that transforms one sequence, like a sentence, into another.
A method for deliberately scrambling or masking parts of input data.
Replacing continuous chunks of text with a single mask token so the model must fill in the blanks.
A component that reads the input both forwards and backwards to capture context.
A component that generates output one token at a time, always moving forward.

cards.title: Rebuild the broken message
body: Learn how BART turns scrambled text into a powerful language model for both understanding and generating text.

cards.title: Why teach a model with puzzles?
body: Imagine giving a student a sentence with missing or jumbled words, then asking them to reconstruct the original. This exercise tests both understanding and creativity.
BART uses this idea to train computers, forcing them to fix corrupted text. By solving these puzzles, the model learns to deeply understand and generate language.

cards.title: How BART learns from chaos
body: First, plain text is deliberately corrupted using shuffling and masking tricks. The model receives this noisy input, with the challenge to reconstruct the original.
The encoder reads the broken text in both directions, gathering clues. The decoder then generates the cleaned-up sequence, token by token.
The outcome is a model that excels at both understanding and creating text, thanks to its training in fixing all kinds of language messes.
cards.caption: BART learns by piecing together disrupted text, building skills for many language tasks.

cards.title: What if you only mask single words?
body: Compare training a model by hiding just one word at a time versus hiding whole spans and shuffling sentences.
cards.offNote: Model learns to fill in the blanks, but may miss bigger context and structure.
cards.onNote: Model learns to reconstruct entire phrases and order, gaining deeper understanding of meaning.

cards.title: BART's central trick: unifying two worlds
body: BART combines the best of two language model families. The encoder reads input in both directions, like BERT, while the decoder generates output step by step, like GPT.
By corrupting input in creative ways and training on reconstruction, BART becomes skilled at both understanding and generating language, all within one architecture.

cards.title: How much should you scramble the input?
body: Adjust the amount of corruption: less makes the task easier, more forces deeper learning, but too much can confuse the model.
cards.mid: Model learns to handle complex disruptions and sentence order.
cards.high: Model may struggle to recover meaning if input is too damaged.

cards.title: Choose BART's ingredients
body: Pick the six core elements that make BART's pretraining unique and effective.

choices.label: Bidirectional encoder
choices.note: Reads input in both directions to capture context.

choices.label: Left-to-right decoder
choices.note: Generates output one token at a time.

choices.label: Sentence shuffling
choices.note: Scrambles sentence order to force learning of structure.

choices.label: Span masking
choices.note: Hides chunks of text, not just single words.

choices.label: Transformer architecture
choices.note: Provides the backbone for flexible encoding and decoding.

choices.label: Denoising objective
choices.note: Trains the model to fix corrupted sequences.

cards.title: BART's impact on language tasks
body: BART set new records in summarization, translation, and dialogue, all using the same pretraining method. It matched or beat specialized models on many benchmarks.
Its flexible design means it can be fine-tuned for a wide range of tasks, making it a go-to choice for both comprehension and generation.

bars.label: Summarization (ROUGE)
bars.note: Up to 6 points higher than previous models

cards.caption: BART's denoising approach raised the bar for both understanding and generating language.

cards.title: Why BART is a landmark model
body: BART showed that one model can master both language comprehension and generation by learning to repair broken messages.
Its unified approach inspired many later models and remains a foundation for modern AI language systems.

recap.label: Corrupt
recap.note: Scramble and mask parts of input text.

recap.label: Encode
recap.note: Read the noisy input in both directions.

recap.label: Decode
recap.note: Reconstruct the original text, step by step.

recap.label: Fine-tune
recap.note: Adapt the model for any language task.

cards.title: BART: Denoising Sequence-to-Sequence Pre-training for Natural Language Generation, Translation, and Comprehension
body: Explore the original paper for technical details, experiments, and full results.
